channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden, Harris thank major Democratic donors and urge them to stay engaged after tough loss to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris on Sunday thanked deep-pocketed Democratic donors who raised record sums in last month's election loss to President-elect Donald Trump and urged them not to lose hope and to remain politically engaged.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Alina Habba? Trump's fierce legal defender picked to serve as counselor to the president
item.description: President-elect Trump announced earlier this month that attorney and adviser Alina Habba will join his administration as counselor to the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top adviser to NYC Mayor Eric Adams abruptly resigns amid federal investigation
item.description: Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a top adviser to NYC Mayor Eric Adams, resigned on Sunday amid a federal investigation into Adams' office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, who had polio as a child, says any of President-elect Donald Trump’s nominees seeking Senate confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: "Speaker Pelosi is enjoying the overwhelming outpouring of prayers and well wishes," Ian Krager said.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats recalibrate their resistance to Trump
item.description: Democrats are not planning an all-hands resistance to President-elect Trump. At least not in 2016 style, when lawmakers, activists, volunteers and millions of angry voters mounted a party-wide effort to curb his newfound influence in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East visit as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump seizes on drone controversy to mock Chris Christie
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked his ex-ally, former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie, Saturday by seizing on the mysterious New Jersey drone controversy on his social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Devin Nunes, Troy Edgar and Bill White
item.description: Trump nominated Truth Social CEO Devin Nunes as chairman of his Intelligence Advisory Board, Troy Edgar as deputy secretary of Homeland Security and Bill White as ambassador to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ABC News to pay $15M in Trump defamation lawsuit
item.description: Court documents publicly filed on Saturday and obtained by NewsNation detailed the settlement's conditions.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drones: State senator wants state of emergency
item.description: One politician in New Jersey is calling for a "limited" state of emergency after what he said is a lack of government transparency over the recent unexplained wave of drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: 5 ways congressional investigations could change in the next Congress and beyond
item.description: The future of congressional investigations could be defined by Republican eagerness to probe private industry and less willingness to defer to the Justice Department, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' from fall on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to turn the clock on daylight saving time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump wants to turn the lights out on daylight saving time. In a post on his social media site Friday, Trump said his party would try to end the practice when he returns to office.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz reflects on Democrats’ shortcomings, return to Minnesota as political ground shifts
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is back at the Capitol and reacclimating to life after his vice presidential bid. Politics Friday host Brian Bakst and senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson unpack an interview with Walz about three months on the national stage and what awaits next year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Dana Ferguson, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Texas bill would allow Bitcoin to be used for taxes, donations
item.description: Texas is among 10 states considering legislation about the expanded use of cryptocurrency by its residents
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to contribute $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund. Meta is also donating $1M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, a move that comes as major tech companies seek to improve their relationship with the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to US and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s most populous province may bar American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Michigan, New York and Minnesota if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump imposes sweeping tariffs on all Canadian products, a senior official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Khanna eyes Defense Department contracting, excess property for DOGE action
item.description: "I want the U.S. to have the greatest military in the world and the resources to counter increasingly sophisticated threats from our adversaries, but we need a more sensible approach," Khanna wrote.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump makes impromptu visit; Secret Service holiday party canceled last minute: Source
item.description: ”Logistics had to be done, everything had to be swept, posts had to be manned, and it just wasn’t possible to have the party Wednesday,” a source said.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. wants daughter-in-law in CIA top role to probe JFK assassination
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. reportedly wants Amaryllis Fox Kennedy and Trump to help release documents about the JFK assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: DFL, Republican senators weigh in on state budget forecast
item.description: The state’s latest economic forecast, revealed Dec. 4, predicts less revenue than expected and higher-than-expected costs for some government programs. MPR News host Emily Bright spoke with state Sens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NJ bill looks to make doxxing a crime
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - When someone is doxxed, it can be more than just a leak of their information; it can rob someone of their well-being, physically and mentally.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk warns Republicans against standing in Trump's way — or his
item.description: Donald Trump’s second term comes with the specter of the world’s richest man serving as his political enforcer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5
